name: Smoked Chicken and Pepperoni Gumbo
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the vegetables: Cut half in larger pieces (about 1/2 inch) and chop the rest finely or use a food processor.
2. Prepare the meats: Use two smoked chicken breasts, pulled from bones, skinned, and cut into bite-sized pieces by hand. If you don't have smoked chicken, use no more than 1/4 teaspoon of liquid smoke. Cut one stick (about 12 inches) of pepperoni into moderately thick slices (about 1/8 inch or twice as thick as regular pizza pepperoni).
3. In a cast iron Dutch oven, sauté large cut onions over medium-high heat in a dry pan for about 3-4 minutes, stirring constantly.
4. Add the rest of the vegetables and cook for about 5 more minutes on medium heat, stirring frequently.
5. Stir in the spice mixture and mix well. Stir in about 2 tablespoons of dry roux.
6. Add the broth and pepperoni slices. Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer for at least half an hour or until vegetables are tender and flavors are blended.
7. About 5 minutes before serving, add the chunks of chicken breasts to the mixture. At the same time, add more roux if desired to thicken.
8. Serve in bowls over rice. For additional smoke flavor, wrap chicken bones and skin in cheesecloth and place in the mixture with the pepperoni. Remove all bones and skin before stirring in the chicken pieces.
Makes 8 to 10 servings.
